title: Comfort At Parting
content:
O little Heart,
    So much I see
    Thy hidden smart,
    So much I long
    To sing some song
    To comfort thee.

    For, little Heart,
    Indeed, indeed,
    The hour to part
    Makes cruel speed;
    Yet, dear, think thou
    How even now,
    With happy haste,
    With eager feet,
    The hour when we
    Again shall meet
    Cometh across the waste.
meaning:
"Comfort At Parting" is a quintessential example of Richard Le Gallienne's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
